mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. Lower cost of capital directly increases present value of corporate cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.28% as of April 2008 reflects ongoing Fed rate cuts from 2007 crisis response
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations. This mechanism operates with a 60-day lag as market participants incorporate employment weakness into forward guidance.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in preceding 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% in April 2008, elevated from 2007 levels
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or Spearman correlation between UNRATE change and 60-day forward returns exceeds -0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth reflects consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. Declining retail sales signal demand weakness that compresses profit margins for consumer-facing firms and reduces aggregate earnings growth, producing negative equity returns within 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 378,148M in April 2008, showing weakness relative to 2007 trend
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of months where RSAFS growth < 0.2%, or Rank IC between RSAFS and 20-day returns exceeds -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth captures real economic activity and manufacturing capacity utilization. When INDPRO declines, it signals supply-side weakness and reduced output capacity, which constrains earnings growth and equity valuations over a 60-day horizon as the market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.18 in April 2008, showing deceleration from prior year
failure_condition: Spearman correlation between INDPRO month-over-month change and 60-day forward SPX returns is positive and exceeds 0.08, or INDPRO declines produce positive returns in > 62% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment and unemployment rate jointly signal labor market health. When payroll growth decelerates while unemployment rises simultaneously, the interaction amplifies the signal of economic weakness, producing sharper equity selloffs within 20 trading days as the market recognizes accelerating job losses.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 0.1% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137,818K and UNRATE at 5.0% in April 2008 reflect labor market stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of months meeting both trigger conditions, or the interaction term adds no predictive power beyond univariate PAYEMS and UNRATE models (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts and retail sales jointly capture consumer sector momentum. When both HOUST and RSAFS decline together, it signals broad-based demand collapse across housing and consumption, producing negative equity returns within 5 trading days as the market reprices recession risk sharply.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month change < -5% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 947K in March 2008, down sharply from 2006-2007 peaks
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 70% of months meeting both trigger conditions, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.10 in logistic regression against direction_5d.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI and monetary policy stance (FEDFUNDS) interact to determine real interest rates. When CPI inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains constrained by crisis response, real rates compress, but if inflation expectations rise faster than Fed accommodation, equity risk premiums expand, producing negative returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 213.74 in April 2008 reflects elevated commodity prices amid monetary accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of months meeting both trigger conditions, or the interaction term does not improve model fit (likelihood ratio test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of aggregate demand. In late-cycle expansions turning to contraction, GDP growth deceleration signals earnings headwinds. The mechanism is regime-dependent: in expansion regimes, GDP deceleration produces negative returns; in recession regimes, the relationship weakens as valuations are already repriced.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,693.1B (Q1 2008) shows deceleration from 2007 growth rates
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion to early contraction transition
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, or the predictive power of GDPC1 is significantly weaker in recession regimes (Rank IC difference > 0.15 between expansion and recession subsamples).
